text.title: Helm Chart Authoring Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the helm-charts methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Set apiVersion: v2, include kubeVersion, list maintainers and use SemVer 2 versioning; never publish a chart without these fields.
rule.rationale: v2 + kubeVersion is the gate between published charts and unusable scratch directories.

rule.statement: Every chart MUST include templates/NOTES.txt, templates/_helpers.tpl and templates/tests/test-connection.yaml.
rule.rationale: NOTES + helpers + test stub is the documented minimum for a chart that survives upgrade and a CI test step.

rule.statement: Provide values.schema.json (JSON Schema draft-07) to validate user values at helm install / upgrade time.
rule.rationale: Schema validation catches typos and missing values before they reach the cluster — orders of magnitude cheaper than runtime debugging.

rule.statement: Pin every dependency to an exact SemVer (never *), commit Chart.lock and gate on helm dependency update + helm lint in CI.
rule.rationale: Wildcards break reproducible builds the day upstream cuts a major; pinned + lock is the single safe pattern.

rule.statement: All configurable values MUST be in values.yaml with comments; no hardcoded values inside templates other than constants documented in NOTES.txt.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded values create branches in the template that cannot be overridden — defeating the chart pattern.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
